item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the president doesn't know about the Foundation, I'd have to assume their funding doesn't come from the government (or at least, not in any part the US government). If that's the case, where does their funding come from? And per your other responses to similar questions, why is the US president inherently less trustworthy than any other yahoo who has to know about the Foundation? Recent years notwithstanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting because it made me laugh. (There seems to be some inconsistencies with the pronoun usage on the final document, particularly the first two paragraphs)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In some iterations of the Foundation, this is true. Not in this one.

I don't believe the Foundation would risk telling elected politicians about the veil.

Can you really trust Trump to not wake up the next day and tweet about the aliens?

Can you trust Bernie Sanders to keep quiet about their rampant human rights abuses?

I don't think so.

That's just what makes the most sense to me so that's the Foundation I portray in my writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, the President knowns about the existence of the SCP Foundation, right? So why don't they just say 'Hello we're with the SCP Foundation, please scratch your nose or else the world is going to die.'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mixed feelings here. I felt like this belong in the -J section, but then again it's a good mainlister. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ever since I saw the concept on the idea forum and when you linked this on irc I loved it. I'm so happy it's on the site because it does a good job at portraying something very inconsequential as difficult/intense when the Foundation has to fix it. The scenes detailing their efforts to get the president to scratch their nose really had me in suspense so great job! I also love the lore and the ghost, the article could work fine without it but it adds a poignant layer to the SCP I didn't expect.

Great concept, great execution, and it manages to be political without being too political, a tough line to navigate so some may disagree. Great job +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why thank you kind gentleperson!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the world will end if the President doesn't scratch their nose…and the ghost responsible for making it itchy is getting dementia?

Man, blending silliness and cosmic horror is so hard to pull off in the SCP universe, but when it works it works. Well done.

item.title: Re: 100+
item.content:encoded: Thank you General o7
Hooray for WizzBlizz! 🎉

item.title: Enthusiastic +1
item.content:encoded: This is just awesome. This is the kind of SCP I absolutely love - a fun, silly thing that can still build tension and have a payoff while staying true to its silliness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is probably the hardest I laughed at an SCP in a while and realizing where the story was going hit just at the right time. Darkly comedic. Easy +1.

Looking forward to more of your works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Open
- Close

How did the Foundation make the correlation between the nose scratching and the meteor?

Meteor came out of nowhere, foundation enacted protocol 3, A5092.amk picked up deviance from normal behaviour in 5092, foundation said fuck it, we have no other leads, let's go scratch his nose.

From Incident-1-A:

At 20:15, Site-10 reported to Overseer Command a deviance of behavior in SCP-5092. (The deviance was picked up by A5092.amk and discovered by personnel during the Protocol 3 checkup).

I have tried to make this a bit clearer based on your feedback though!

How did they make the connection between the nose itching and the ghost?

I mean, it's just a connection they are investigating, but like, it's pretty obvious. The ghost disappears every day at the exact same point that the nose scratch occurs.

such as not using caricatures of political figures

I agree the Biden dialogue was not meant to be taken seriously, but personally, I think the Trump dialogue was quite good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Others have already said most of this, but

1: You had a great short and sweet article on the first page. Just a weird event the Foundation is keeping an eye. It's the exact kind of content that is missing.
2: Adding insanely high stakes completely destroyed the suspension of disbelief. How did the Foundation make the correlation between the nose scratching and the meteor? How did they make the connection between the nose itching and the ghost? Why would the nose thing be connected to anything at all? You don't have to explain everything, but you should explain something.
3: The dialogue was off. Improvements to the dialogue, such as not using caricatures of political figures, would make this far easier to read and also have it feel more grounded.

-1 for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoileridoos
Spoileridoos

For the past 600 odd years, the ghost has been using his hat-feather to prevent 5092-B events by tickling the President's nose at 7:53. (who the curse affected before the USA existed is intentionally ambiguous)

However, recently the ghost has begun developing spectro-cognitive degradation disorder and has on multiple occasions forgotten to go tickle the President's nose, hence the recent 5092-B events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the connection between the ghost and the SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you will find that I named it sniffle squad actually smh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: scp-2762! A carved stone snake, in the style of ancient Mesoamerican cultures. Every full moon at solar midnight as experienced by the President of the US, it sucks up 10 cubic meters of matter then disgorges it in the form of a 17 meter long animate snake that tries to kill and eat the president.

item.title: Re: fixed
item.content:encoded: Haha don't worry I also cannot read :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautiful, just beautiful

+1

item.title: Re: fixed
item.content:encoded: Welp, apparently this time it was just me. Forgive me, sometimes I appear to be unable to read

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for sure. Managed to make me snicker and also kept a serious and urgent tone. Love the implications at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I still fondly remember that stone created by the Soviet GRU that creates giant snakes that try to kill the current President of the United States.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the first page:

Each hour, all recorded footage is stored automatically in terminal 12, Site-10. It will then be fed through an algorithm (A5092.amk) to check for any deviances from SCP-5092's standard behavior. The Algorithm should be checked for faults annually.

I have clarified this a bit based on your feedback though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey thanks for taking the time to leave a comment! I have made a few changes based on what you said.

item.title: Re: fixed
item.content:encoded:
Uh. 5092-1 materialised at 19:53 and Theta-20 achieved the mission goal about 30 minutes later…

Area-32 reported -1's materialisation at 19:55. 1hr and 15m later is 21:10.

item.title: Re: fixed
item.content:encoded:
[…] would collide in 1hr and 15m at 21:10.

At 20:19, MTF Theta-20 successfully neutralized the threat.

At 20:21, Area-32 reported that SCP-5092-1 had dematerialized.

Is it just me or these times still don't check?

item.title: fixed
item.content:encoded:
Man I KNEW I would mess up the numbers somewhere…
Thanks for pointing this out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, the big question I have about this, is how was this correlation even discovered? Obviously, the Foundation would notice the giant meteor, but how would they possibly discover it was related to the president scratching his nose, an action no one is likely to pay any attention to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is indeed one of the best articles I've read, although I have some concerns with Incident 5092-1-B…

[…] would collide in 1hr and 15m at 21:10.

At 21:38, MTF Theta-20 successfully neutralized the threat.

At 20:07, Area-32 reported that SCP-5092-1 had dematerialized.

Is it just me or those times don't check?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So you’re telling me that the Foundation has the ability to erase a siege on the White House from the global consciousness using a single task force but can’t just engineer a meme that will cause the President to itch and scratch his nose at the required time? This article falls into a specific class of frustrating where I have to accept a cascading number of weird gotchas that simply don’t have a good in-universe justification and baldly exist to force the Foundation to do some exactingly dumb thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate you taking the time to read my article and leave your thoughts! I guess I'll say a few things:

Stuff
No more stuff?

A feather tickling device to make the president scratch his nose in his sleep?

If it works, it works. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Furthermore, for me to accept the world of this article, I have to decide that, not only is the president kept in the dark about the Foundation for some reason

Okay, so, (in my headcanon at least), the foundation CANNOT tell elected officials about the veil or the anomalous. What if Trump wakes up one day and decides to tweet about the aliens? What if Bernie Sanders decides to tell the world about their D Class experiments? The veil is fragile, and they can't risk giving information to anyone aside from their most trusted.

Foundation agents somehow both casually stroll into the White House in one event while rooftop snipers start shooting at them in another, which shows off the lack of even internal consistency.

Well, these were two entirely different situations. The first situation was just an ordinary day with ordinary security. In the second one, they were on extremely high security. The White House was barricaded, and the President was escorted to a secure bunker that's literally designed to protect the president from an aerial attack on Washington.

Despite having SS credentials, they were denied access and had to forcefully bypass the police line. When they literally beat their way through, the WH assumed they were being attacked by some kind of paramilitary organization, hence the response. (And as stated, their communications were jammed by agents embedded in the WH)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really wanted to like this. As has been pointed out, there are some pretty top tier lines in this article, such as "Obama, scratch your fucking nose!" It's clear that there was a lot of thought put into the article, and the fundamental premise is brilliant.

Unfortunately, this article is weighed down by so much extra cruft that it hides the real gems like those lines. The dialogue for most characters comes across as such a caricature of real life that it goes beyond silly into so absurd as to break suspension of disbelief. A feather tickling device to make the president scratch his nose in his sleep? That's beyond "wacky;" it's nonsensical.

Furthermore, for me to accept the world of this article, I have to decide that, not only is the president kept in the dark about the Foundation for some reason, this is also a universe where the Foundation resorts to slapstick hijinks, full frontal assaults on the White House, and fake phone calls rather than any sensible solution, which is too far for me. Foundation agents somehow both casually stroll into the White House in one event while rooftop snipers start shooting at them in another, which shows off the lack of even internal consistency.

Honestly, I originally thought the article was going to end at just the first page, and I dreaded seeing a second. The first page was so brilliantly bizarre that I loved it. There was enough there to work as a perfect unexplained mystery, and the premise suggests so much more than is said. The reveal that this nose scratching is fundamentally vital to our continued existence brought it down in my opinion, because it revealed too much behind the curtain-but it still would have been fine had the rest of the implementation not brought it down. All in all, it's a very promising idea that falls flat for me in its execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how it went from a safe class nose itch to a keter asteroid-crashcourse-resettimer.
Good use of MTF, hell you even named one "Sniffle Squad".

Props on the good dialogue which includes: a believable call, Joe Biden - Obama bromance, Trump's way of addressing things
.
The way you edited two pictures together to make T-128C is also very nice.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There have been a lot of SCPs featuring presidents. Other than Ronald Reagan Cut Up While Talking, this is probably my favorite. "Obama, scratch your fucking nose!" is an iconic line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that tension was legitimately built over the president scratching their nose is a good feat of writing. I can excuse some of the problems this skip has for the raw concept and execution alone.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was very iffy on this, but I think I'm going to upvote. While incredibly, incredibly silly, this is by far not the most absurd thing on the site to be taken with a grain of seriousness (not even the only one involving the goddamn president). Additionally, random phenomenon correlation is always a fun avenue for SCPs, and I think this article pulls the 180 turnaround spectacularly. +1

item.title: Happy +1
item.content:encoded:
Just like during critique I think that this is a phenomenal idea and I love it as an entry! This is exactly the kind of short, weird anomaly that made me fall in love with the wiki originally!

Plus it’s hilarious as hell and I just love it for all that it is

item.title: Re: big +1
item.content:encoded: Thank you unbiased stranger!

item.title: big +1
item.content:encoded: very good story, no bias.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked this? Check out SCP-5083!

And there she is: My first SCP. I hope you enjoy it!

Thank you to Riemann, Grigori Karpin, RockTeethMothEyes, and forbiddenquest for helping me flesh out my idea,
and a BIG thank you to:

  • Mr Thomas Anderson
  • Grigori Karpin (again)
  • Pedagon
  • ItsDenali

For helping me iron out my draft!

(Also thank you to RhettyBoy for giving my MTF the name "Sniffle Squad" among other stuff)

Feel free to drop any suggestions for improvement below!
